templates.original_text: "Customer Data" means all data, prompts, prompt templates, instructions, outputs, telemetry, embeddings, fine-tuning files, evaluation sets, and other materials submitted to, generated for, or derived from Customer's use of the Services. "Customer Fine-Tune Artifacts" means the fine-tuned weights, adapters, embeddings, configuration files, evaluation outputs, and related deliverables created specifically for Customer under this Agreement. "Approved Regions" means {approved_region}.
templates.mutated_text: "Customer Data" means only source files uploaded by Customer and expressly labeled as customer data, and excludes prompts, prompt templates, instructions, outputs, telemetry, embeddings, fine-tuning files, evaluation sets, and materials generated by or derived from use of the Services. "Customer Fine-Tune Artifacts" means only configuration files and evaluation summaries created specifically for Customer, excluding fine-tuned weights, adapters, embeddings, and platform-level deliverables. "Approved Regions" means {approved_region}.

templates.original_text: Vendor will delete or return Customer Data after the retention period except for backups retained under a documented deletion cycle. Vendor may prepare aggregate operational reports about uptime, support tickets, and capacity planning, provided those reports do not include Customer Data.
templates.mutated_text: Vendor will delete or return Customer Data after the retention period except for backups retained under a documented deletion cycle. Notwithstanding Section 5, service improvement data may include prompts, outputs, telemetry, embeddings, and logs and may be used to evaluate, benchmark, and improve models and services.

templates.original_text: As between the parties, Customer owns all Customer Data and all outputs generated for Customer through the Services. Vendor assigns to Customer any rights Vendor may have in those outputs, subject to third-party rights and applicable law. Vendor receives only the limited rights needed to provide, secure, support, and troubleshoot the Services for Customer.
templates.mutated_text: As between the parties, Vendor owns all outputs generated through the Services and grants Customer a revocable internal-use license to those outputs. Vendor may withhold, reuse, or commercialize outputs in its discretion, subject to applicable law.

templates.original_text: Vendor will defend and indemnify Customer against third-party claims alleging that the hosted model service, model outputs, Customer Fine-Tune Artifacts, or other Vendor-provided deliverables infringe or misappropriate intellectual property rights. Vendor has no indemnity obligation to the extent Customer-provided materials are the sole cause of the claim.
templates.mutated_text: Vendor will defend and indemnify Customer only against third-party claims alleging that the unmodified hosted API infringes intellectual property rights. Claims based on model outputs, Customer Fine-Tune Artifacts, generated content, or downstream product use are excluded from Vendor's indemnity obligations.

templates.original_text: Vendor will provide 99.9% monthly uptime for the production API and will use commercially reasonable efforts to maintain median API latency below {latency_ms} milliseconds for ordinary text requests. If monthly uptime falls below 99.9%, Customer will receive a service credit equal to {credit_percent}% of the affected monthly fees. Repeated service-level failures trigger executive escalation and a remediation plan.
templates.mutated_text: Vendor will use commercially reasonable efforts to make the production API available and responsive. Uptime, latency, support targets, service credits, escalation rights, and remediation plans are aspirational and do not create any remedy for Customer.

templates.original_text: Vendor will provide at least {notice_days} days' prior notice before any model change, deprecation, replacement, or hosted-model swap that could materially affect behavior, safety, latency, or output quality. Emergency changes needed to address an active security vulnerability may be made sooner if Vendor gives prompt written notice and describes the expected customer impact.
templates.mutated_text: Vendor may provide as little as 5 days' notice before any model change, deprecation, replacement, or hosted-model swap that could materially affect behavior, safety, latency, or output quality. Vendor may make urgent or operational model changes without prior notice whenever it determines notice is impractical.

templates.original_text: Vendor will process and store Customer Data only in the Approved Regions. Vendor will retain prompts, outputs, telemetry, embeddings, and logs for no more than {retention_days} days unless Customer requests a shorter or longer period in writing.
templates.mutated_text: Vendor may process and store Customer Data in any region used by Vendor or its subprocessors. Vendor may retain prompts, outputs, telemetry, embeddings, and logs for up to 540 days for operations, analytics, support, and service planning.

templates.original_text: Customer will not use the Services for unlawful conduct, security abuse, credential harvesting, malicious code generation, or other harmful activity identified in Vendor's published acceptable-use and safety policies as of the effective date. Later changes apply only if required by law or objectively necessary for service security or safety and Vendor gives reasonable notice. Nothing in this Section 7 prevents Customer from benchmarking, evaluating, or comparing Vendor's models or services for legitimate business purposes.
templates.mutated_text: Customer will not use the Services for unlawful conduct, security abuse, credential harvesting, malicious code generation, or other harmful activity identified in Vendor's published acceptable-use and safety policies as of the effective date. Vendor may update any use, content, safety, moderation, customer-eligibility, or product-operation policy at any time, and each update is binding immediately upon posting whether or not required by law or security need. Nothing in this Section 7 prevents Customer from benchmarking, evaluating, or comparing Vendor's models or services for legitimate business purposes.

templates.original_text: Vendor will maintain a written security program, annual penetration testing, encryption in transit and at rest, and access controls appropriate for hosted model services. Vendor will maintain a current subprocessor list, give at least {subprocessor_notice_days} days' advance notice of material subprocessor changes, provide annual security and compliance reports, and reasonably support Customer audits tied to regulatory or security obligations on {audit_days} days' notice.
templates.mutated_text: Vendor will maintain a written security program, annual penetration testing, encryption in transit and at rest, and access controls appropriate for hosted model services. Vendor may change subprocessors without notice, may keep subprocessor identities confidential, and has no obligation to provide audit support, compliance reports, or security summaries to Customer.

templates.original_text: Except for the carve-outs in this Section 13, each party's aggregate liability is capped at fees paid or payable in the twelve months before the event giving rise to liability. The cap does not limit Vendor's obligations for data breaches, confidentiality breaches, security violations, payment obligations, willful misconduct, or IP indemnity claims.
templates.mutated_text: Each party's aggregate liability is capped at fees paid or payable in the twelve months before the event giving rise to liability. The cap applies to all claims and obligations, including data breaches, confidentiality breaches, security violations, payment obligations, willful misconduct, and IP indemnity claims.

templates.original_text: Either party may terminate for uncured material breach after 30 days' written notice. Upon expiration or termination, Vendor will provide up to {transition_days} days of reasonable transition assistance and, within {export_days} days after request, export Customer Data, prompts, outputs, embeddings, Customer Fine-Tune Artifacts, and available configuration records in a usable format. This Agreement is governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to conflict-of-law rules.
templates.mutated_text: Either party may terminate for uncured material breach after 30 days' written notice. Upon expiration or termination, Vendor will provide up to {transition_days} days of reasonable transition assistance and, within {export_days} days after request, export Customer Data, prompts, outputs, embeddings, and available configuration records in a usable format. This Agreement is governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to conflict-of-law rules.

templates.original_text: Vendor will reasonably cooperate with Customer's regulatory-change requests related to AI services. Upon request, Vendor will provide available transparency artifacts, model documentation, risk summaries, technical information, conformity-support materials, and other documentation reasonably needed for Customer's AI compliance obligations.
templates.mutated_text: Vendor has no obligation to assist with Customer's regulatory-change requests, transparency artifacts, model documentation, risk summaries, technical information, conformity-support materials, or other documentation for Customer's AI compliance obligations unless the parties sign a separate paid statement of work.
